questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Watch for deer crossing the road
Never drive in this lane
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Come to a complete stop
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Come to a complete stop
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
U-turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Left turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Right turns are not allowed
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Left turns are not allowed

Parking reserved for disabled permit holders

Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red

Do not drive in this direction
Right turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Right turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction

Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
U-turns are not allowed
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane

Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment

Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Start of a no-passing zone
The road ahead is divided by a median

Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Start of a no-passing zone
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead

An intersection is ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median

Two lanes are merging into one

questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck

Two lanes are merging into one

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall

Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment

Watch for deer crossing the road
A railroad crossing is ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A series of curves ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
Never drive in this lane
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

An intersection is ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing

A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
A railroad crossing is ahead

An intersection is ahead
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down

Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Sharp right then left turns ahead
Never drive in this lane
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

The road ahead is divided by a median

choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed

Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
A series of curves ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Road construction or detour ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady red traffic light mean?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Come to a complete stop and remain stopped until the light changes
Stop only if other vehicles are present
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A steady red light requires a full stop behind the stop line or crosswalk until the signal turns green.

questions.prompt: What should you do at a steady yellow traffic light?
choices: Stop if you can do so safely, because the light is about to turn red
Always stop immediately no matter what
Speed up to beat the red light
Treat it the same as a green light
questions.explanation: A steady yellow warns the red is coming, so stop if you can do so safely rather than rushing through.

questions.prompt: A flashing red traffic light should be treated like which of the following?
choices: A stop sign
A yield sign
A green light
A speed limit sign
questions.explanation: A flashing red light means stop completely, then proceed when it is safe, just like at a stop sign.

questions.prompt: What does an eight-sided (octagonal) red sign always mean?
choices: Stop
Yield
Do not enter
Speed limit
questions.explanation: The octagon shape is reserved only for stop signs, requiring a complete stop.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing yellow traffic light mean?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop completely before entering
The light is broken and should be ignored
Make a U-turn
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light warns you to slow down and proceed carefully through the intersection.

questions.prompt: What is the typical meaning of a diamond-shaped, yellow road sign?
choices: It warns of a hazard or changing road conditions ahead
It gives directions to a city
It marks a construction zone
It prohibits parking
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond signs are warning signs that alert drivers to hazards such as curves, intersections, or merges ahead.

questions.prompt: What color are signs used in highway construction and work zones?
choices: Orange
Blue
Green
Brown
questions.explanation: Orange signs and markings indicate construction and maintenance work zones, where you should slow down and stay alert.

questions.prompt: A solid yellow line on your side of the centerline means what?
choices: You may not pass
You may pass if traffic is clear
The road is one-way
You must turn left
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited because it is unsafe to cross.

questions.prompt: A green arrow signal pointing left means what?
choices: You may turn left and oncoming traffic is stopped for you
You must yield to all oncoming traffic before turning
You may not turn left
You must stop before turning
questions.explanation: A green arrow gives you a protected turn, meaning conflicting traffic is being held by a red light.

questions.prompt: What do white lane lines on a roadway separate?
choices: Lanes of traffic moving in the same direction
Traffic moving in opposite directions
The road from the shoulder only
Parking spaces
questions.explanation: White lines separate lanes traveling the same direction, while yellow lines separate opposing traffic.

questions.prompt: At an intersection with no signals, two vehicles arrive at the same time. Who must yield?
choices: The driver on the left yields to the driver on the right
The driver on the right yields to the driver on the left
The faster vehicle goes first
The larger vehicle goes first
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach an uncontrolled intersection together, the driver on the left must yield to the one on the right.

questions.prompt: When you want to turn left at an intersection, you must do what?
choices: Yield to oncoming traffic and pedestrians before turning
Turn quickly before oncoming cars arrive
Assume oncoming drivers will stop for you
Honk and proceed
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians crossing the road being entered.

questions.prompt: In Indiana, when may you turn right on a red light?
choices: After a complete stop, when it is safe and not prohibited by a sign
At any time without stopping
Only when a green arrow appears
Never
questions.explanation: Right turn on red is allowed after a full stop and yielding, unless a posted sign prohibits it.

questions.prompt: When approaching a roundabout, what must you do?
choices: Yield to traffic already in the roundabout and enter when it is safe
Stop and wait for the roundabout to be completely empty
Enter quickly without yielding
Come to a stop in the center
questions.explanation: Drivers entering a roundabout must yield to vehicles already circulating and merge into a safe gap.

questions.prompt: You are about to enter a road from a private driveway. What should you do?
choices: Yield to all traffic and pedestrians already on the road
Proceed because vehicles on the road must yield to you
Speed up to merge quickly
Stop only if you see a car
questions.explanation: Vehicles entering a roadway from a driveway or alley must yield to traffic and pedestrians already there.

questions.prompt: An emergency vehicle is approaching with lights and siren. What must you do?
choices: Pull over to the right edge of the road and stop until it passes
Speed up to get out of the way
Stop immediately in your lane
Continue at the same speed
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: Who has the right-of-way at a crosswalk?
choices: Pedestrians in or entering the crosswalk
Whichever vehicle arrives first
Turning vehicles
Faster-moving traffic
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield to pedestrians in or entering a crosswalk.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, who proceeds first?
choices: The vehicle that arrived and stopped first
The vehicle on the left
The largest vehicle
Whoever accelerates fastest
questions.explanation: At a four-way stop the first vehicle to fully stop has the right to go first.

questions.prompt: Before making any turn, you should signal at least how far in advance?
choices: Well before the turn so other drivers have time to react
Only as you begin the turn
After completing the turn
Signaling is optional
questions.explanation: Signaling early gives other drivers and pedestrians time to anticipate your move.

questions.prompt: When making a right turn, you should position your vehicle where?
choices: Close to the right curb or edge of the road
In the center of the road
In the left lane
As far left as possible
questions.explanation: Right turns should be made from as close to the right curb as practical to avoid cutting off other traffic.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the default speed limit in an Indiana urban or residential district?
choices: 30 mph
20 mph
45 mph
55 mph
questions.explanation: Indiana's default speed limit in urban and residential districts is 30 mph unless signs say otherwise.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is Indiana's default maximum speed on a rural interstate highway?
choices: 70 mph
55 mph
65 mph
80 mph
questions.explanation: Indiana's default maximum on rural interstate highways is 70 mph unless a lower limit is posted.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the default speed limit on most rural Indiana state highways?
choices: 55 mph
45 mph
65 mph
35 mph
questions.explanation: Most rural state highways in Indiana have a default limit of 55 mph unless signs indicate otherwise.

questions.prompt: What does the basic speed law require?
choices: You must never drive faster than is reasonable and safe for conditions
You may always drive the posted limit regardless of weather
You must drive exactly the speed limit at all times
Speed is unlimited if no sign is posted
questions.explanation: The basic speed law requires adjusting your speed to road, weather, and traffic conditions, even below the posted limit.

questions.prompt: What is a good minimum following distance under ideal conditions?
choices: At least three seconds behind the vehicle ahead
One car length total
Half a second
As close as possible
questions.explanation: The three-second rule provides enough time to react and stop safely in good conditions.

questions.prompt: In rain, snow, or other poor conditions, how should you adjust your following distance?
choices: Increase it to four seconds or more
Keep it the same
Decrease it to save time
Eliminate it and tailgate
questions.explanation: Poor conditions lengthen stopping distance, so you should increase following distance to four seconds or more.

questions.prompt: What is the speed limit in an alley in Indiana unless otherwise posted?
choices: 15 mph
25 mph
30 mph
10 mph
questions.explanation: Indiana law sets the default speed in alleys at 15 mph.

questions.prompt: Why should you slow down before entering a curve?
choices: So you do not lose control or run off the road
Because curves have no speed limit
To save fuel
Because brakes do not work in curves
questions.explanation: Slowing before a curve helps you keep traction and control rather than braking sharply mid-curve.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic can be dangerous because it can do what?
choices: Block the flow of traffic and cause crashes
Always improve safety
Reduce fuel use to zero
Make passing impossible for everyone
questions.explanation: Driving too slowly impedes traffic flow and can lead to dangerous passing and rear-end crashes.

questions.prompt: What is the safest way to manage the space around your vehicle?
choices: Keep space on all sides and an escape path when possible
Only worry about the space in front
Stay in another vehicle's blind spot
Drive as close to other cars as you can
questions.explanation: Managing space on all sides gives you room to maneuver if traffic suddenly stops or swerves.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, what must you always check in addition to your mirrors?
choices: Your blind spot over your shoulder
Only the rearview mirror
Nothing, mirrors show everything
The radio
questions.explanation: Mirrors do not show the blind spot beside you, so a shoulder check is needed before changing lanes.

questions.prompt: On a multilane highway, the left lane should generally be used for what?
choices: Passing slower vehicles
Driving slowly
Parking
Turning right
questions.explanation: Slower traffic should keep right, and the left lane is intended mainly for passing.

questions.prompt: When is passing prohibited?
choices: On hills or curves where you cannot see far enough ahead
On any straight road
When the road is dry
Whenever there is a broken yellow line
questions.explanation: You must not pass where limited sight distance, such as a hill or curve, makes it unsafe.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, when is it safe to return to your lane?
choices: When you can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
Immediately after pulling alongside
Before fully passing the vehicle
Only after the other driver waves
questions.explanation: Seeing the passed vehicle in your mirror means you have enough room to return safely.

questions.prompt: When another vehicle is passing you, what should you do?
choices: Maintain or slightly reduce your speed
Speed up to prevent the pass
Swerve into their lane
Brake hard
questions.explanation: Holding or easing your speed lets the passing driver complete the maneuver safely.

questions.prompt: Passing is generally not allowed in which of the following locations?
choices: At intersections and railroad crossings
On open straight highways
In a passing lane
On wide rural roads
questions.explanation: Passing at intersections and railroad crossings is prohibited because of the high risk of conflict.

questions.prompt: What should you do before every lane change?
choices: Signal your intention to other drivers
Change lanes without warning
Honk continuously
Turn off your headlights
questions.explanation: Signaling tells nearby drivers what you plan to do and reduces the chance of a collision.

questions.prompt: When passing a large truck, you should remember that the truck driver cannot see you if you cannot see what?
choices: The truck driver's mirrors
Your own headlights
The truck's tires
The road behind you
questions.explanation: If you cannot see the truck driver's mirrors, you are in a blind spot and the driver cannot see you.

questions.prompt: A broken white line between lanes means what?
choices: Lane changes are permitted when safe
Lane changes are never allowed
The road ends ahead
You must stop
questions.explanation: A broken white line separates same-direction lanes and allows lane changes when it is safe.

questions.prompt: Indiana's Move Over law requires drivers approaching a stopped emergency vehicle with flashing lights to do what?
choices: Slow down and move over a lane when possible
Speed up past the vehicle
Stop in the middle of the road
Turn off their headlights
questions.explanation: The Move Over law requires slowing and, when safe, changing lanes to protect stopped emergency and service workers.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with a motorcycle, you should do what?
choices: Give it a full lane and allow extra following distance
Share the lane beside it
Pass closely to encourage it to speed up
Follow it more closely than a car
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are entitled to a full lane and need extra space because they are small and stop quickly.

questions.prompt: When you must stop behind a school bus with flashing red lights and an extended stop arm, what should you do?
choices: Stop and remain stopped until the lights stop flashing and the arm folds in
Pass quickly on the left
Honk and drive around
Slow down but keep moving
questions.explanation: Flashing red lights and an extended stop arm mean children are loading or unloading and you must stay stopped.

questions.prompt: What is a no-zone with respect to large trucks?
choices: A blind spot where the truck driver cannot see your vehicle
A lane reserved for trucks
A no-parking area
A high-speed lane
questions.explanation: No-zones are the large blind spots around trucks where your vehicle is not visible to the driver.

questions.prompt: When passing a bicyclist, you should do what?
choices: Pass at a safe distance and only when it is clear
Pass as closely as possible
Honk to move them off the road
Refuse to share the road
questions.explanation: Bicyclists have the right to use the road, so pass them with plenty of room when it is safe.

questions.prompt: When you encounter slow-moving farm equipment on a rural road, you should do what?
choices: Slow down and pass only when it is safe and legal
Pass immediately regardless of oncoming traffic
Tailgate to encourage it to move
Pass on a curve
questions.explanation: Farm equipment moves slowly, so reduce speed and pass only with clear sight distance and a legal passing zone.

questions.prompt: Near schools, parks, and residential areas, you should do what?
choices: Slow down and watch for children who may enter the road
Maintain highway speed
Assume children will stay on the sidewalk
Use your horn continuously
questions.explanation: Children can be unpredictable, so reduce speed and stay alert near schools, parks, and neighborhoods.

questions.prompt: Why should you avoid lingering alongside a large truck?
choices: Trucks have large blind spots and need room to maneuver
Trucks travel faster than cars
It is illegal to drive near trucks
Trucks cannot turn
questions.explanation: Staying beside a truck keeps you in a blind spot and in danger if the truck needs to change lanes.

questions.prompt: When a pedestrian who is blind is crossing with a white cane or guide dog, you must do what?
choices: Stop and yield the right-of-way
Honk to alert them
Drive around quickly
Proceed because vehicles have priority
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield to pedestrians, and especially to those using a white cane or guide dog.

questions.prompt: In Indiana, what is the illegal blood alcohol concentration (BAC) for a driver age 21 or older?
choices: 0.08 percent or higher
0.05 percent or higher
0.10 percent or higher
0.02 percent or higher
questions.explanation: A driver 21 or older is legally intoxicated at a BAC of 0.08 percent or higher in Indiana.

questions.prompt: Under Indiana's zero-tolerance law, a driver under 21 can be cited for alcohol with a BAC as low as what?
choices: 0.02 percent
0.08 percent
0.05 percent
0.10 percent
questions.explanation: Indiana's zero-tolerance law makes it illegal for drivers under 21 to drive with a BAC of 0.02 percent or more.

questions.prompt: Under Indiana's hands-free law, what is illegal while driving?
choices: Holding or using a phone in your hand
Using a hands-free or mounted device
Talking to a passenger
Listening to the radio
questions.explanation: Since 2020, Indiana law prohibits holding a phone or device in your hand while driving; only hands-free use is allowed.

questions.prompt: How does alcohol affect a driver?
choices: It slows reaction time and impairs judgment
It improves reaction time
It has no effect at low amounts
It sharpens vision
questions.explanation: Even small amounts of alcohol slow reactions and impair judgment, increasing crash risk.

questions.prompt: What is the safest way to handle a phone call while driving?
choices: Use hands-free technology or wait until you are parked
Hold the phone and talk
Text instead of calling
Dial while steering with your knees
questions.explanation: Hands-free use or pulling over keeps your hands on the wheel and attention on driving.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an example of a driving distraction?
choices: Texting, eating, or adjusting controls while driving
Keeping both hands on the wheel
Scanning the road ahead
Checking your mirrors regularly
questions.explanation: Anything that takes your eyes, hands, or mind off driving, such as texting or eating, is a distraction.

questions.prompt: Can prescription or over-the-counter medications impair your driving?
choices: Yes, some medicines can cause drowsiness or slowed reactions
No, only alcohol impairs driving
Only illegal drugs impair driving
No, medicine always improves driving
questions.explanation: Many legal medications can impair driving, so check labels and warnings before driving.

questions.prompt: What is the only thing that can sober up a person who has been drinking?
choices: Time
Coffee
A cold shower
Exercise
questions.explanation: Only time allows the body to remove alcohol; coffee and other tricks do not lower BAC.

questions.prompt: If you feel drowsy while driving, what is the safest action?
choices: Pull over to a safe place and rest
Turn up the radio and keep driving
Drive faster to finish the trip
Open a window and continue for hours
questions.explanation: Drowsiness impairs driving like alcohol, so the safest choice is to stop and rest.

questions.prompt: In Indiana, when must you turn on your headlights in relation to your windshield wipers?
choices: Whenever your wipers are in continuous use
Only at night
Never with wipers on
Only in heavy snow
questions.explanation: Indiana law requires headlights to be on whenever your windshield wipers are operating continuously in rain or snow.

questions.prompt: When is a road often most slippery during rainfall?
choices: During the first rain after a dry period
After several hours of heavy rain
Only when it freezes
When the road is completely flooded
questions.explanation: The first rain lifts oil and dirt to the surface, creating an especially slick film on the road.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle starts to skid, what should you do?
choices: Ease off the gas and steer in the direction you want to go
Slam on the brakes
Steer sharply in the opposite direction
Accelerate hard
questions.explanation: Easing off the accelerator and steering toward your intended path helps you regain control of a skid.

questions.prompt: What should you use in fog to see and be seen?
choices: Low-beam headlights
High-beam headlights
No lights
Hazard lights only while moving normally
questions.explanation: High beams reflect off fog and reduce visibility, so use low beams in foggy conditions.

questions.prompt: What is hydroplaning?
choices: When tires ride on a film of water and lose contact with the road
When brakes overheat
When the engine stalls in rain
When headlights fog up
questions.explanation: Hydroplaning occurs when water builds between the tires and the road, causing a loss of traction.

questions.prompt: When you must dim your high beams for an oncoming vehicle, you should switch to low beams within how many feet?
choices: 500 feet
100 feet
1,000 feet
50 feet
questions.explanation: You must dim high beams within 500 feet of an oncoming vehicle to avoid blinding the other driver.

questions.prompt: How should you adjust your driving at night?
choices: Slow down because you can see only as far as your headlights reach
Drive faster to spend less time in the dark
Use high beams in all situations
Follow other cars closely for light
questions.explanation: At night your visibility is limited to your headlights, so reducing speed keeps you within your sight distance.

questions.prompt: On icy or snow-covered roads, how should you brake?
choices: Gently and gradually to avoid skidding
Hard and suddenly
Only with the parking brake
Not at all
questions.explanation: Gentle, gradual braking helps maintain traction and prevents skids on slippery surfaces.

questions.prompt: Where is parking prohibited?
choices: In front of a fire hydrant
In a marked parking space
In a public parking lot
Along an open curb with no signs
questions.explanation: Parking is prohibited in front of fire hydrants and other locations that block access or visibility.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, which way should you turn your front wheels?
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Turning wheels toward the curb downhill lets the curb stop the car if it rolls forward.

questions.prompt: When parking uphill next to a curb, which way should you turn your front wheels?
choices: Away from the curb
Toward the curb
Straight ahead
Whichever way is easier
questions.explanation: Turning wheels away from the curb uphill lets the wheel catch the curb if the car rolls back.

questions.prompt: What should you always do after parking your vehicle?
choices: Set the parking brake
Leave the engine running
Leave it in neutral on a hill
Leave the doors open
questions.explanation: Setting the parking brake keeps the vehicle from rolling, especially on a slope.

questions.prompt: What does a sign reading 'Do Not Enter' mean?
choices: You may not drive past the sign in that direction
You may enter cautiously
Enter only at night
Yield before entering
questions.explanation: A 'Do Not Enter' sign means traffic is prohibited from entering, often marking a one-way or exit ramp.

questions.prompt: On a one-way street, in which direction does all traffic move?
choices: In a single direction only
In both directions
Whichever direction is faster
Only toward intersections
questions.explanation: All traffic on a one-way street moves in the same single direction indicated by signs and markings.

questions.prompt: You may not stop or park where?
choices: Within an intersection or on a crosswalk
In a legal parking space
On your own driveway
In a parking garage
questions.explanation: Stopping or parking in an intersection or crosswalk blocks traffic and pedestrians and is prohibited.

questions.prompt: Before opening your door into traffic after parking, you should do what?
choices: Check for approaching vehicles and bicycles
Open it quickly to save time
Assume no one is coming
Honk first
questions.explanation: Checking traffic before opening your door prevents collisions with passing vehicles and cyclists.

questions.prompt: In Indiana, at what minimum age can you get a learner's permit if you are enrolled in an approved driver education course?
questions.explanation: Indiana allows a learner's permit at age 15 when enrolled in an approved driver education program.

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised driving practice does Indiana require before a probationary license?
choices: At least 50 hours, including 10 at night
At least 20 hours
At least 100 hours
No specific number
questions.explanation: Indiana requires at least 50 hours of supervised practice, with at least 10 of those hours at night.

questions.prompt: While driving with a learner's permit, who must be in the vehicle with you?
choices: A licensed driver who meets the age requirement, seated in the front
Anyone with a permit
No one is required
Only a parent over 30
questions.explanation: A learner's permit holder must be supervised by a qualifying licensed driver in the front passenger seat.

questions.prompt: During the first 180 days with a probationary license, a new driver under 21 generally may not drive during which hours?
choices: Between 10 p.m. and 5 a.m.
Between noon and 5 p.m.
Between 6 a.m. and 9 a.m.
There is no time restriction
questions.explanation: For the first 180 days, probationary drivers may not drive between 10 p.m. and 5 a.m. except for specific exemptions.

questions.prompt: During the early restriction period, a new probationary driver under 21 may carry passengers only if what is true?
choices: They are immediate family or a qualifying licensed adult is present
There are no passengers at all
The passengers are also teens
The vehicle has four doors
questions.explanation: For the first 180 days, passengers are limited to immediate family unless a qualifying licensed adult rides along.

questions.prompt: What should you do if you move to a new address?
choices: Update your address with the BMV
Do nothing
Wait until your license expires
Only tell your insurance company
questions.explanation: You must keep your address current with the BMV so official notices reach you.

questions.prompt: What must you do when a law enforcement officer asks for your license?
choices: Present your license or permit
Refuse to show it
Drive away
Show only your registration
questions.explanation: Drivers must carry and present their license or permit to law enforcement when requested.

questions.prompt: What can happen if you violate the restrictions on your learner's permit or probationary license?
choices: Your driving privileges can be suspended
Nothing happens
You automatically get a full license
Your restrictions are removed early
questions.explanation: Violating permit or probationary restrictions can lead to suspension and delay full licensing.

questions.prompt: Under Indiana's implied consent law, what have you agreed to by driving?
choices: To submit to a chemical test for alcohol or drugs if lawfully requested
To never be tested
To pay all future fines in advance
To waive your license
questions.explanation: By driving in Indiana you consent to chemical testing, and refusal results in an automatic license suspension.

questions.prompt: Indiana's seat belt law is a primary offense, which means what?
choices: An officer can stop and ticket you solely for not wearing a seat belt
You can only be ticketed after another violation
Seat belts are optional
Only the driver must buckle up
questions.explanation: As a primary offense, failing to wear a seat belt is itself enough reason for an officer to stop and cite you.

questions.prompt: In Indiana, children under what age must be secured in a child restraint system?
choices: Under age 8
Under age 4
Under age 12
Under age 2
questions.explanation: Indiana law requires children under age 8 to be properly secured in a child restraint system based on their size.

questions.prompt: What can result from accumulating too many points on your Indiana driving record?
choices: License suspension
A higher speed limit
Automatic license renewal
Removal of all restrictions
questions.explanation: Points add up for traffic violations, and too many can lead to suspension of your license.

questions.prompt: What happens if you refuse to take a chemical test when lawfully requested in Indiana?
choices: Your license is automatically suspended
Nothing happens
You receive a warning only
You get a discount on fines
questions.explanation: Refusing a lawful chemical test triggers an automatic license suspension under implied consent.

questions.prompt: What should you do when a police officer is directing traffic at an intersection with working signals?
choices: Obey the officer's directions even if they differ from the signal
Follow the signal and ignore the officer
Stop and wait for the signal only
Drive through without stopping
questions.explanation: Directions from a law enforcement officer override traffic signals and signs.

questions.prompt: A conviction for operating while intoxicated (OWI) in Indiana can result in what?
choices: Fines, license suspension, and possible jail
A warning with no penalty
Automatic dismissal
Only points on your record
questions.explanation: OWI is a serious offense carrying fines, suspension, and the possibility of jail time.

questions.prompt: If you are involved in a crash, what should you do?
choices: Stop, help if needed, and exchange information
Leave the scene immediately
Move the cars and forget it
Refuse to share information
questions.explanation: You must stop at a crash, render aid if needed, and exchange information; leaving the scene is illegal.

questions.prompt: Who must wear a seat belt in a moving vehicle in Indiana?
choices: The driver and all required occupants
Only the driver
Only children
Only back-seat passengers
questions.explanation: Indiana requires the driver and occupants to be properly restrained, with seat belt use enforced as a primary offense.
